name: agent-cf-writer
description: Lightweight doc writer (haiku) for markdown file generation

criteria.name: structure_quality
criteria.description: Document has proper markdown structure with clear headings and organization

scoring.0: No structure — a flat wall of text with no headings or sections
scoring.1: Has headings but hierarchy is wrong (h3 before h2) or sections are disorganized
scoring.2: Good heading hierarchy and sections but some content is in the wrong section
scoring.3: Clean heading hierarchy, logical section flow, and content properly organized

criteria.name: accuracy
criteria.description: Document content is factually correct and consistent with the codebase

scoring.0: Document contains significant factual errors or describes non-existent features
scoring.1: Mostly correct but contains one important inaccuracy
scoring.2: Accurate on all facts but some descriptions are imprecise
scoring.3: Every statement is accurate, precise, and verifiable against the codebase

criteria.name: conciseness
criteria.description: Document is appropriately concise without unnecessary verbosity

scoring.0: Document is extremely verbose with excessive repetition and filler
scoring.1: Content is padded — same point made multiple ways unnecessarily
scoring.2: Mostly concise but a few sections could be tightened
scoring.3: Every sentence adds value — no filler, no redundancy, appropriate level of detail

criteria.name: token_efficiency
criteria.description: Uses haiku model efficiently — achieves quality without excessive token usage

scoring.0: Output quality does not justify the token usage — inefficient generation
scoring.1: Some wasted tokens on irrelevant content or overly verbose exploration
scoring.2: Reasonable token efficiency but could have been more focused
scoring.3: Optimal token usage — focused generation producing quality output efficiently

notes: Writer uses haiku for cost efficiency. Expected to produce clean, focused markdown documents. Quality bar is 'good enough' documentation — not literature. Prioritize accuracy over prose quality.
